该轮融资由总部位于伦敦的成长型股票基金Kennet Partners牵头。此次加薪反映了在线活动的蓬勃发展，总部位于伦敦的初创公司Hopin去年筹集了4000万美元的A轮融资。成立于2016年的Grip吸引了一些大型活动的组织者作为客户，其中包括Reed Exhibitions和Messe Frankfurt。
 Grip首席执行官兼创始人Tim Groot在一份声明中说：“我们的使命是使组织者有权将专业人员聚集在一起，以促进行业发展。这一轮融资将使我们能够利用我们广泛的行业领先平台，将体验提升到一个新的水平，为虚拟，混合和个人活动提供独特的价值。”
  他说，他们现在将对该产品进行大量投资，并寻求全球扩张。
 过去，Grip的其他竞争对手包括Brella筹集了150万美元，Swapcard迄今为止筹集了600万美元。
 那么，为什么Grip似乎以这种方式脱离了包装呢？
 Groot告诉我：“我们采取了稍微不同的方法，因为我们设法与其他平台一起使用即插即用方法。因此，许多组织者都将握把用作独立的虚拟事件平台。因此，他们可以将Hopin用于会议，而将Grip用于网络。因此，也许我们可以在2020年期间以这种方式获得更大的吸引力。”
 在2020年，随着虚拟事件的发展，Grip每月举办100多个事件，有150万人使用。因此，该公司表示，到2020年，收入将增长近4倍，而今年，该公司预计将在其平台上举办超过10,000个活动，参与者将超过500万。 
Grips AI驱动的算法意味着与会者可以根据自己的兴趣（包括赛前会议安排）获得更多个性化的婚介推荐。对于参展商，该软件可捕获业务线索并提供事后分析。
 可以将人们添加到会议中以进行小组对话，并且该初创公司还致力于基于主题的“快速联网”功能，以进行3分钟的即时对话。
 Grip与诸如Vimeo，Youtube，Zoom，BlueJeans等各种流媒体平台集成，与Hopin或Bizzabo等“全方位服务”平台不同。
 Kennet的合伙人兼Grip董事会成员Hillel Zidel补充说：“ Grip能够组织虚拟活动，并且重点关注网络，这意味着该公司在过去一年中取得了巨大的增长。尽管面对面的活动受到限制，活动组织者及其客户仍能够与他们的客户保持联系。随着现场活动在未来的恢复，Grip处于非常有利的位置，可以通过提供支持现场，虚拟和混合活动的软件解决方案来继续协助活动组织者。”
 Founders Factory联合创始人，之前的投资者Brent Hoberman表示：“ Grip的诞生源于我们在Founders Forum举办活动中看到的需求–您如何使用智能技术促进来宾之间最相关，最有价值的联系？”
note_en:
The round was led by London-based growth equity fund Kennet Partners. The raise is reflective of the boom in online events, which saw London-based startup Hopin raise a $40m Series A last year. Founded in 2016, Grip counts some large event organizers as clients including Reed Exhibitions and Messe Frankfurt.
 In a statement Tim Groot, CEO and founder of Grip, said: “Our mission is to empower organizers to bring professionals together to advance industries. This funding round is going to enable us to take the experience to a new level, leveraging our extensive industry-leading platform, offering unique value for Virtual, Hybrid and In-Person events.”
  He said they would now be investing heavily in the product and looking to global expansion.
 Other competitors to Grip have, in the past, included  Brella which raised $1.5m, and  Swapcard which raised $6m to date.
 So why is it that Grip seems to have pulled away from pack in this way?
 Groot told me: “We took a slightly different approach in that we managed to work in a plug-and-play method alongside other platforms. So grip gets used as a standalone virtual event platform by lots of these organizers. So they might use Hopin for the conference but Grip for the networking. So maybe we managed to get more traction that way, over the course of 2020.”
 In 2020, following the pivot to virtual events, Grip hosted over 100 events a month and was used by 1.5m people. As a result, the company says revenue grew almost 4x in 2020, and this year it expects to do over 10,000 events on its platform with over 5 million participants.
 Grips AI-powered algorithms mean attendees get more personalized matchmaking recommendations based on their interests, including pre-event meeting scheduling. For exhibitors, the software captures business leads and provides post-event analytics.
 People can be added to meetings to have group conversations and the startup is also working on a topic-based “speed networking” functionality to hold instant 3 minute conversations.
 Grip integrates with various streaming platforms such as Vimeo, Youtube, Zoom, BlueJeans and others, unlike “full-service” platforms such as Hopin or Bizzabo.
 Hillel Zidel, Partner at Kennet and Grip board member, added: “Grip’s ability to organize virtual events with a key focus on networking has meant that the company has seen tremendous growth over the last year. Event organizers and their clients have been able to remain connected with their customers despite the constraints on in-person events. As live events resume in the future, Grip is extremely well-positioned to continue to assist event organizers through the provision of software solutions supporting live, virtual and hybrid events.”
 Brent Hoberman, co-founder of Founders Factory and a previous investor, said: “Grip was born out of a need we saw in running events at Founders Forum – how do you use smart technology to catalyze the most relevant and valuable connections between your guests?”
title: 事件网络应用程序Grip筹集了1300万美元，因为大流行迫使事件保持虚拟
title_en: Event networking app Grip raises $13M, as pandemic forces events to stay virtual

之前，我详细介绍了印度为什么要购买比特币。在这里，我们将讨论为什么印度实际上不能禁止比特币，而应该使用其现有的外汇制度（FEMA）将加密货币视为一种外币。我们的论点分为四个部分：
  简而言之，由于技术，社会和政治原因，不能真正禁止使用加密货币。这项禁令的支持者认为，这可能有助于印度的国家安全，但会发现，一项加密禁令只是关闭了光线充足，受监管的交易所，而支持地下加密，并造成数万亿美元的附带损害。坏演员不会遵守禁令，有能力这样做的人会把自己或自己的钱转移到国外，而且每年有数亿普通印度人因从这个新的金融互联网中防火墙而遭受的经济损失不断增加。
 该决定很重要，印度公众尚未意识到其中的风险。如果该国将加密货币合法化，那么在2020年代，资产增值和GDP增长的积极影响可能达到数万亿美元，因为加密货币的价值已超过1T，并且增长迅速。相反，＆＃34;禁令＆＃34;从长远来看，随着金融互联网在国外的发展，印度将花费数万亿美元。
 印度应该代替加密禁令，使加密合法化，利用其对汇款友好的性质，并通过扩展《外汇管理法》（FEMA）将加密货币视为外国资产来解决潜在的滥用行为。相比之下，禁令将使印度回到失败的自由化前的FERA时代，当时怀疑外国资产，贸易受到抑制。
  现在，让我们更详细地讲，从为什么我们使用术语“禁令”开始。用引号引起来。
   这在社会上是不可行的：杰出的工程师，亿万富翁金融家以及全球数千万加密货币持有者面临着巨大的障碍。
 这在政治上是不可行的：其他国家/地区将推出自己的国家数字货币，而印度将无法阻止它们这样做，也不能阻止它们将其产品国际化。 
要了解为什么禁止使用比特币在技术上不可行，让我们从您可能了解的东西开始：Gmail。您使用自己的地址（例如yourname@gmail.com）和密码（例如lkajsd834908）登录Gmail。任何人都可以将电子邮件发送到该地址，但是只有您（使用密码）才能从该地址发送电子邮件。
 比特币是相似的。您有一个地址（如3FZbgi29cpjq2GjdwV8eyHuJJnkLtktZc5）和一个密码短语（如忘记翼跟随燕子实现正确的观察晚餐见证混合动力）。任何人都可以将比特币（BTC）单位发送到您的地址，但是只有您（带有密码短语）才能从该地址发送BTC。
 但是，有一个很大的区别。使用Gmail，如果您忘记了密码，Google可以重置它。使用比特币，如果您忘记了密码，则没人可以重置它。这样做的缺点是您可能会完全失去比特币。好处是，没有人（包括Google）可以访问您的密码，这意味着没有人可以移动您的BTC。
  一旦您意识到比特币密码短语实际上只是代表任何数量的BTC的几个词，您就会意识到从个人手中夺取BTC的意义上实际上禁止比特币是多么困难。每个人的财产都可以存储在一个很小的文本文件中，硬盘搜索无法进行。跨边界运输一些单词是微不足道的。这些单词可以隐藏在图像中，也可以离线存储。而存储数年的单词并不需要持续的互联网连接甚至是电源。您实际上可以在纸屑上存储数十亿美元的BTC。
 那是个人持有的BTC。整个全球比特币网络，即促进人与人之间BTC转移的计算机网络又如何呢？这也很难攻击。比特币区块链已在全球数百万台计算机上复制，并且受到密码保护，以防止任何尝试创建新货币，重写交易历史记录或更改帐户余额的尝试。比特币采矿和节点分布在许多地理区域，并且大多位于印度以外。
  那只是比特币。还有其他加密货币，例如以太坊。即使这些加密货币之一失败了，它们也不大可能全部以相同的方式失败，因为它们使用了不同的算法。例如，要破坏工作量证明货币，可以尝试捕获位于不同国家/地区的物理采矿场。但是，要损害权益证明货币，您需要捕获世界各地大量的通常为假名的个人。
 而且，我们甚至还没有获得过去几年中部署的所有新技术，例如去中心化交易所，隐私权币和跨链互操作性。所有这些使加密货币作为一类（不仅仅是比特币）难以被抓住或关闭。 
总结一下：专门针对加密货币的比特币和更广泛的加密货币都是为了抵制禁令。要让政府真正阻止印度人将比特币交易仅靠互联网关闭一天，一周甚至一年就足够了。取消货币化的经验也不适用，因为比特币的发行者不是印度政府，而且法定命令不会将BTC的价值降低为零。
 为了真正防止印第安人持有BTC或发送BTC交易，该州需要（a）使该国遭受足以破坏所有形式的存储器的电磁脉冲或大量消磁，（b）无限期禁止所有移民和移民，以及（c）完全停止与国外人员进行一切形式的交流（包括邮寄），以防止任何人向印度发出几句话。
   从社会角度来看，比特币和更广泛的加密货币现在拥有约1亿用户，并获得了世界科技和金融精英的广泛支持。我们正在谈论亿万富翁，首席执行官，密码学家，工程师，创始人，企业家，风险资本家和天使投资者。随着越来越多的人权活动家，运动员，名人和影响者。
  这些都是足智多谋，相互联系的国际移动人们，他们多年来一直在为可能限制加密货币做准备。他们中的许多人在意识形态上都致力于加密货币的成功，早在加密货币没有像现在这样具有经济影响力之前。许多其他公司拥有数十亿美元的业务，这取决于对加密货币的持续和扩展的法律接受度。鉴于加密货币是其身份，资产净值和/或业务的重要组成部分，因此必须执行“禁令”。一个国家的移民只会导致迁移到另一个住所。这包括持有加密货币的印度创始人，企业家和风投，否则他们将在印度投资或开展业务。
  在社会上，印度人将通过向海外转移以创办加密公司或投资于现在有加密货币敞口的任何外国实体（例如MSTR或特斯拉）中的一项禁令。因此，从社会角度来看，所有＆＃34; ban＆＃34;这样做的确增加了印度人持有加密货币的成本，并阻止了加密货币投资者和创始人进入印度。
  国家不能禁止加密的最有趣的原因可能是他们不能禁止其他国家。因为我们即将进入全球货币竞争的新时代。
 回想一下，2002年Google新闻发布时，它突然使每家报纸相互竞争。地理垄断开始消失。没有以当地新闻的方式大量发表的当地报纸发现，与其他所有人一样，重印相同的AP标题已不再是一种可行的商业模式。 
随着多个国家开始推出本国数字货币，并且从100M +加密货币所有者发展到1B +，同一件事将在2020年代发生。每个国家都将在集中控制与全球采用本国数字货币之间，在权力与金钱之间进行权衡。
 国家数字货币的明显设计是简单地由一个仅由政府用户控制的中央数据库来支持它，监视每笔交易，要求所有国内交易都使用它，并禁止所有竞争的数字货币以刺激采用。这类似于印度报道的使用数字卢比的行动方案。
 但是，这种明显的方法立即引发了一个问题：一个试图迫使其公民在国内采用本国数字货币的国家，由于以下原因，将很快需要面对无休止的国际货币竞争这一现实。
 1.首先，其他国家将继续存在，并且还将发行本国数字货币。谈论国家数字货币的不仅是中国和印度。其他许多州也是如此。这些国家将继续存在，因为甚至没有一个超级大国可以入侵世界或控制每个人的货币政策。
 2.第二，某些国家/地区希望将其本国数字货币国际化，使其在国外持有。货币国际化是指在其发行国境外广泛使用一种货币。自2000年代末以来，中国人一直在寻求人民币的国际化，并且印度内部正在就是否将卢比国际化进行讨论。货币国际化既有成本也有收益，但让我们假设，至少某些国家将希望使本国货币国际化，以获得其本国货币在国外被广泛接受和持有的经济利益和软实力。
 3.第三，一些国家将促进本国数字货币与加密货币之间的交易。这已经是事实，因为我们看到金融中心和技术之都，如新加坡，迪拜，瑞士，迈阿密和爱沙尼亚正在拥抱加密货币。原因很明显：加密货币为采用加密货币的国家带来了巨大的经济利益。这些金融中心将促进本国数字货币和加密货币的国际交换。

 4.第四，国家不能轻易阻止其公民在其计算机上持有外国数字货币或加密货币。从设计上讲，加密货币和其他数字资产是所谓的“承载工具”。用户在笔记本电脑上拥有其加密货币的私钥。至于国家数字货币，我们尚不知道中国的数字人民币将如何运作或其他国家将如何运作。但是，如果像已被广泛采用的基于美元的稳定币那样实施任何国家数字货币，它们也将允许用户在本地持有等效的API密钥，同时保留区块链级别的管理权限以在特殊情况下使用。在此模型中，您将在计算机或电话上拥有一个表示“数字现金”的文件。以本国货币表示，哪些应用程序可以用于正常交易，但是在特殊情况下，发行国可以冻结或撤销。
 5.第五，其他国家/地区不会授予其本国数字货币的根访问权限。巴西不允许印度管理数字货币，就像印度不允许巴西管理数字卢比一样。国家数字货币的全部目的是货币主权。
 因此，综合考虑这五点，就说明了为什么禁止加密不可行的政治原理：这意味着禁止从外国使用本国数字货币。
  当您结合技术，社会和政治优势时，很显然，任何试图禁止加密货币的国家/地区都可以。将无法成功禁止其边界之外的加密货币。禁令发布后，去中心化加密货币将继续存在，并且至少在瑞士和爱沙尼亚等一些国家/地区是合法的。禁令后，其他州的国家数字货币也将继续存在。如果发生禁令，人们将移居国外或购买外国实体的权益，以获得去中心化加密货币和其他本国数字货币的敞口。
  国际环境影响国内环境。许多分散式加密货币网络拥有数百万用户，并且拥有与小民族国家一样大的用户群。实际上，每个这样的网络都希望“外国人”持有其加密货币，以增加其市值和采用率。许多国家的数字货币将是本应由外国人持有的国际化的国家数字货币，例如数字瑞士法郎。

 货币政策。资产是否像比特币一样具有稳定且可预测的货币政策？例如，您可以预测几年后的通货膨胀率吗？
 可逆性。资产容错吗？是否可以与系统管理员联系以进行电汇等冲销交易？ 
审查审查抗性。资产审查是否可以抵抗，从而没有系统管理员可以像比特币转移那样撤消任何交易？
 挥发性。资产的价值每天是否在变化，还是像“稳定币”那样相对稳定？或国家数字货币？
 注意，强制仅仅是此特征矩阵中的一个特征。也就是说，国内加密货币禁令是一种激励。但这并不是一个完全确定的方法，特别是当您考虑到改变生命的10000X +上行空间来自持有比特币或以太坊等加密资产时，以及在至少某些司法管辖区加密货币在国外仍然合法的事实。如果印度让技术专家在持有加密货币和在印度运营之间做出选择，那么许多人会选择持有加密货币。
 总结：由于印度实际上无法在其边界以外禁止分散式加密货币和本国数字货币，因此，印度将不得不在禁令上展开更多竞争。数字卢比将不得不在其功能上竞争。它将需要超越所有其他国家的数字货币和加密货币才能被广泛采用。它不能仅凭胁迫就获胜。全世界聪明的政客都明白这一点。甚至纽约市也必须采用加密货币来维持其如今作为金融之都的地位。
  作为世界金融之都纽约市市长，我将投资使这座城市成为BTC和其他加密货币的枢纽。
-安德鲁·杨（AndrewYang🧢🗽🇺🇸）（@AndrewYang）2021年2月11日

note_en:
Previously, I detailed why India should  buy Bitcoin. Here we'll discuss why India cannot actually ban Bitcoin, and should instead treat crypto as a foreign currency using its existing foreign exchange regime ( FEMA). Our argument proceeds in four parts:
  In short, crypto cannot truly be banned for technical, social, and political reasons. The proponents of a ban think it could help Indian national security, but would find that a crypto ban simply shuts down well-lit, regulated exchanges in favor of underground crypto, and causes trillions in collateral damage to boot. Bad actors would not obey a ban, people with the means to do so would move themselves or their money abroad, and hundreds of millions of ordinary Indians would suffer compounding economic losses for every year they are firewalled from this new  financial internet.
 The decision is important and the Indian public does not yet appreciate the stakes. If the country legalizes crypto, the positive impact may amount to trillions in both asset appreciation and GDP growth over the 2020s, because crypto is already worth >$1T and growing fast. Conversely, a "ban" would cost India trillions over the long term as the financial internet grows outside the country.
 Instead of a "ban", India should legalize crypto, take advantage of its remittance-friendly properties, and resolve potential misuse by extending the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) to treat cryptocurrencies as foreign assets. By contrast, a ban would revert India to the failed pre-liberalization  FERA era, when foreign assets were suspect and trade was inhibited.
  Let's now go into more detail, starting with why we put the term "ban" in quotes.
   It's not socially feasible: brilliant engineers, billionaire financiers, and tens of millions of crypto holders globally present formidable obstacles.
 It's not politically feasible: other states will be launching their own national digital currencies, and India will not be able to prevent them from doing so nor stop them from internationalizing their offerings.
  To understand why banning Bitcoin is not technically feasible, let's start with something you probably understand: Gmail. You log into Gmail with your address (like  yourname@gmail.com) and a password (like  lkajsd834908). Anyone can send email to that address, but only you – with the password – can send email from that address.
 Bitcoin is similar. You have an address (like  3FZbgi29cpjq2GjdwV8eyHuJJnkLtktZc5) and a  passphrase (like  forget wing follow flip swallow achieve correct view dinner witness hybrid proud). Anyone can send units of Bitcoin (BTC) to your address, but only you – with the passphrase – can send BTC from that address.
 There is one big difference, though. With Gmail, if you forget your password, Google can reset it. With Bitcoin, if you forget your passphrase,  no one can reset it. The downside of this is that you can lose your Bitcoin entirely. The upside is that  no one else (including Google) has access to your passphrase, and that means no one else can move your BTC.
  Once you realize that Bitcoin passphrases are literally just a few words that can represent  any amount of BTC, you realize how hard it is to actually ban Bitcoin in the sense of seizing BTC from individuals. Each person's holdings can be stored on a tiny text file that no hard drive search will be able to pick up. Transporting a few words across borders is trivial. The words can be  hidden in images, and they can be stored offline. And storing a few words for years does not require persistent internet connectivity or even power. You can literally store billions of dollars of BTC on a scrap of paper.
 That's BTC held by individuals. What about the global Bitcoin network as a whole, the network of computers that facilitates transfers of BTC  between people? That's tough to attack too. The Bitcoin blockchain is replicated on millions of computers worldwide, and  cryptographically shielded against any attempt to create new currency, rewrite transaction history, or change account balances. Bitcoin mining and nodes are distributed across many geographic regions, and are situated mostly outside India.
  And that's just Bitcoin. There are other cryptocurrencies, like Ethereum. And even if one of these cryptocurrencies fails, it's unlikely they all fail  in the same way, because they use different kinds of algorithms. For example, to compromise a  proof-of-work currency one might try capturing the physical mining farms located in different countries. But to compromise a  proof-of-stake currency you'd need to capture a critical mass of often-pseudonymous individuals around the world.
 Moreover, we haven't even gotten to all the new technologies that have been deployed over the last few years like decentralized exchanges, privacy coins, and cross-chain interoperability. All of these make cryptocurrencies as a class (beyond just Bitcoin) harder to seize or shut down.
 To summarize: Bitcoin specifically and cryptocurrencies more generally were built to resist bans. For the government to truly stop Indians from making Bitcoin transactions a mere internet shutdown for a day or week or even a year would not be enough. The experience of  demonetization is also not applicable, because the issuer of Bitcoin is not the Indian government, and a fiat decree will not reduce the value of BTC to zero.
 To really prevent Indians from holding BTC or sending BTC transactions, the state would need to (a) subject the country to an  electromagnetic pulse or  mass degaussing sufficient to destroy all forms of memory storage, (b) ban all immigration and emigration indefinitely, and (c) stop all forms of communication entirely with people outside the country (including physical mail) to prevent anyone from sending a few words out of India.
   From a social perspective, Bitcoin specifically and cryptocurrency more generally now boasts ~100M users and enjoys broad support among the world's tech and financial elite. We're talking about billionaires, CEOs, cryptographers, engineers, founders, entrepreneurs, venture capitalists, and angel investors. Along with an increasing number of human rights activists, athletes, celebrities, and influencers.
  These are resourceful, connected, internationally mobile people who have been preparing for possible restrictions on cryptocurrency for many years. Many of them are ideologically dedicated to the success of cryptocurrency, well before it was as economically impactful as it is today. Many others have billion dollar businesses which are dependent upon the continued and expanded legal acceptance of cryptocurrency. Given that crypto is a significant part of their identity, net worth, and/or business, a "ban" in one country will just result in relocation to another domicile. This includes crypto-holding Indian founders, entrepreneurs, and VCs who would otherwise have invested in or done business in India.
  Socially, Indians will respond to a ban by moving overseas to found crypto companies or investing in one of the any foreign entities that now have exposure to cryptocurrency (like  MSTR or  Tesla). So from a social perspective, all a "ban" does is increase the cost of holding crypto for Indians, and stop crypto investors and founders from entering India.
  Perhaps the most interesting reason that countries can't ban crypto is that they can't ban other countries. Because we are about to enter a new age of global monetary competition.
 Recall that when  Google News launched in 2002, it suddenly pitted every newspaper against each other. Geographic monopolies began evaporating. Local newspapers that didn't publish much in the way of local news found that reprinting the same AP headline as everyone else was no longer a viable business model.
  The same thing is about to happen over the 2020s as  multiple  countries begin launching national digital currencies and we go from  100M+ cryptocurrency owners to 1B+. Each country will face a tradeoff between centralized control and global adoption of its national digital currency, between power and money.
 The obvious design for a national digital currency is to simply back it by a centralized database which only approved government users control, to surveil every transaction, to require it for all domestic transactions, and to ban all competing digital currencies to spur adoption. This is similar to India's  reported course of action with the digital rupee.
 But this obvious approach immediately runs into a problem: a state that tries forcing its citizens to adopt a national digital currency domestically will soon need to contend with the reality of  unbannable international currency competition, for the following reasons.
 1. First, other countries will continue to exist, and will also issue national digital currencies. It isn't just China and India that are talking about national digital currencies. So are many other states. And these states will continue to exist, as not even a superpower can invade the world or control everyone's monetary policy.
 2. Second, some nations will want to  internationalize their national digital currency such that it is held abroad.  Currency internationalization is the widespread use of a currency outside the borders of its country of issue. The Chinese have sought to  internationalize the RMB since the late 2000s, and there is discussion within India over whether to  internationalize the rupee. There are both  costs and benefits to currency internationalization, but let us assume that at least  some nations will want to internationalize their currency for the economic benefits and soft power of having their national currency widely accepted and held abroad.
 3. Third, some countries will facilitate trades between national digital currencies and cryptocurrencies. This is already true, as we see financial centers and technology capitals like Singapore, Dubai, Switzerland, Miami, and Estonia embracing crypto. The reason is obvious: crypto generates enormous  economic benefits for the nations that adopt it. And these financial centers will facilitate the international exchange of national digital currencies and cryptocurrencies.
 The City of Miami believes in  #Bitcoin and I’m working day and night to turn Miami into a hub for crypto innovation.   Proud to say Miami is the first municipal government to host Satoshi’s White Paper on government site.  @balajis  @tyler  @cameron  @APompliano  pic.twitter.com/DBOni23ynY
— Mayor Francis Suarez (@FrancisSuarez)  January 27, 2021
 4. Fourth, nations can't easily stop their citizens from holding foreign digital currencies or cryptocurrencies on their computers. Cryptocurrencies and other digital assets are, by their design, so-called "bearer instruments". A user holds the private keys to their cryptocurrency on their laptops. As for national digital currencies, we don't yet know how the Chinese digital yuan will work or what other countries will do. However, if any national digital currencies are implemented like the USD-based  stablecoins that are already widely adopted, they would also allow users to hold the equivalent of an  API key locally while retaining administrative rights at the blockchain level to use under exceptional circumstances. In this model, you'd have a file on your computer or phone which represents "digital cash" in a national currency, and which applications could use for normal transactions, but which the issuing state could freeze or reverse in extraordinary circumstances.
 5. Fifth, nations won't give other nations root access over their own national digital currencies. Brazil wouldn't let India administer the digital real, just like India wouldn't let Brazil administer the digital rupee. The entire purpose of a national digital currency is monetary sovereignty.
 So, taken together these five points outline the  political rationale for why banning crypto is not feasible: it would mean banning national digital currencies from foreign states.
  When you combine the technical, social, and political vantage points, it becomes clear that any country that tries to "ban crypto" will not succeed in banning crypto beyond its borders. Decentralized cryptocurrencies will continue to exist after any "ban", and will be legal in at least some countries like Switzerland and Estonia. National digital currencies for other states will also continue to exist after a "ban". And in the event of a "ban", people will move abroad or buy interests in foreign entities to gain exposure to decentralized cryptocurrencies and other national digital currencies.
  The international context affects the domestic context. Many decentralized cryptocurrency networks have millions of users, and have userbases as large as small nation states. Virtually every such network wants "foreigners" to hold its cryptocurrency in order to grow its market capitalization and adoption. And many national digital currencies will be   internationalized national digital currencies which are meant to be held by foreigners, like a digital Swiss franc.
 We can see a sneak preview of what a crypto "ban" would look like in Nigeria. Ministers realized it couldn't be stopped, and are now talking about turning things around.
  “We didn’t create Cryptocurrency and so we cannot kill it and cannot also refuse to ensure it works for us. These children are doing great business with it and they are getting result and Nigeria cannot immune itself from this sort of business." - Senator Biodun Olujimi
— The Nigerian Senate (@NGRSenate)  February 11, 2021
  Because all these other cryptocurrencies and foreign digital currencies will be accessible over the internet, we are entering an era of  global monetary competition. Over the next ten years hundreds of millions of people will own many different kinds of digital assets, including cryptocurrencies, foreign national digital currencies, and the national digital currency of their own state.
 All of these will  compete against each other, because it will be  easy for someone to hold a portfolio of (say) BTC, ETH, digital stocks and bonds, the Brazilian digital real, and the Indian digital rupee on their own computer. The digital rupee will not be able to win this competition with a simple ban, because people will choose their portfolio of currencies based on  several characteristics.
 Coercion. Does the state require them to hold it? This is the impact of a ban, but compliance with any ban cannot be assumed to be 100%.
 Upside. Does the asset offer the upside of a Bitcoin or Ethereum, which have appreciated  10000X+ over the last decade? If this is life-changing enough, then people will change their lives to get around a ban by moving abroad or buying shares in overseas vehicles with crypto exposure.
 Adoption. Can they buy relevant goods and services with it? Is it used by people in their social network?
 Privacy. Does the currency protect privacy? Note that for the same reason Signal is  drawing users from WhatsApp, private money will tend to outcompete surveillance money. In other words, there is a real economic cost to financial surveillance.
 Transparency. Does the asset allow for cryptographically verifiable transparency, to facilitate accounting and financial statements?
 Multisignature. Does the asset allow multiple users to sign transactions? For example, to facilitate disbursement of funds upon a corporate board vote.
 Programmability. Does the digital asset facilitate programmability, such as the use of Ethereum-based smart contracts? This is a very important use case that facilitates new kinds of programs and protocols.
 Scalability. Does the asset support a large number of transactions per second, as is required for applications like micropayments?
 Monetary Policy. Does the asset have a stable and predictable monetary policy, like Bitcoin? For example, can you predict the inflation rate several years out?
 Reversibility. Is the asset error-tolerant? Is there a system administrator that can be contacted to reverse transactions, as with a wire transfer?
 Censorship-Resistance. Is the asset censorship-resistant, such that there is  no system administrator that can reverse any transactions, as with a Bitcoin transfer?
 Volatility. Does the asset change in value from day-to-day, or is it relatively stable like a "stablecoin" or national digital currency?
 Note that coercion is just  one feature in this feature matrix. That is, a domestic crypto "ban"  is an incentive. But it's not a fully determining one, particularly when you consider the life-changing 10000X+ upside that comes from holding crypto assets like Bitcoin or Ethereum, and the fact that crypto will remain legal abroad in at least some jurisdictions. If India makes technologists choose between holding crypto and operating in India, many will choose to hold crypto.
 In summary: because India can't actually ban decentralized cryptocurrencies and national digital currencies beyond its borders, it will have to compete on more than just a ban. The digital rupee will have to compete on its  features. It will need to  outcompete all other national digital currencies and cryptocurrencies to be widely adopted. It won't be able to win solely on the basis of coercion. Smart politicians around the world understand this; even New York City must adopt crypto to maintain its status as a financial capital now.
  As mayor of NYC - the world’s financial capital - I would invest in making the city a hub for BTC and other cryptocurrencies.
— Andrew Yang🧢🗽🇺🇸 (@AndrewYang)  February 11, 2021
  Now let's shift gears for a second. Why do people  want to resort to coercion, to a ban in the first place? What do they think it  could do? Let's empathize with them.
 We don't yet have draft language for today's proposed ban, but the  draft bill from 2019 lists four proposed reasons to ban crypto currencies. To paraphrase, here's what people who want a ban in 2019 were concerned with:
 National security. Bad actors are sending money into India to stir up violence and agitation, and fund terrorism. Just like demonetization, we want to cut off dark money flows and a crypto ban, even if imperfect, will help with that.
 Consumer protection. The volatility of cryptocurrencies means that people can lose money, and we need to protect consumers from downside.
 Energy consumption. The energy consumption of proof-of-work currencies like Bitcoin is very high, and we want to save energy.
 Monetary policy. Because they can't be controlled by the government, cryptocurrencies themselves imperil monetary policy and national sovereignty.
 Proponents of the 2021 ban have added an important new wrinkle. They say that you can do blockchain without crypto, with a digital rupee on a  permissioned ledger that has none of these issues. Their thinking is that bad actors can't use digital rupees as easily because the RBI would have root access, the rupee is stable, a permissioned ledger doesn't consume energy via Bitcoin-like mining, and it integrates directly with the country's monetary policy. So, their belief is that if you  could ban all digital currencies, you  could just get the good features with a digital rupee with none of the bad, and solve the national security, consumer protection, energy consumption, and monetary policy issues listed above.
  The issue is that while a digital rupee  is indeed a good idea, none of these four desired goals will be achieved with a "ban".
   National security: a ban hits good actors, not bad ones. First, as detailed above, you  won't be able to ban bad actors from sending and receiving cryptocurrencies because (a) at the human level, bad

1984年是计算领域具有里程碑意义的一年。当然，这是Macintosh的首次亮相，但它也催生了另一款永恒的计算机硬件：IBM Model M键盘，Matt Neuburg认为这是有史以来最伟大的键盘（请参阅“有史以来最伟大的计算机键盘”）。 ？”（2009年2月27日）。
 M型代表了为满足打字机用户而设计的悠久的键盘时代，其特征在于令人满意的可点击的翘曲弹簧键。 1992年，IBM将大部分键盘制造业务转移给利盟，后者继续为Big Blue生产Model M键盘。最终，该行业转向了像橡胶穹顶这样的更便宜，更笨拙的机制，而利盟和IBM在1990年代后期退出了M型业务。但是，一群利盟员工购买了位于肯塔基州列克星敦的工厂，并组建了Unicomp。
 Clicky键盘曾经是一种小众产品，近年来又重新流行起来。现在它们如此流行，主流公司提供了机械“游戏”键盘，您可以在许多实体店甚至沃尔玛那里购买。同时，自从苹果公司明显倒闭以来，Unicomp就一直在生产那些经典的Model M clicky键盘。
 Unicomp甚至生产Mac专用的型号，Spacesaver M，但我不愿意推荐它。这是因为，多年来，旧的Lexmark工具已经破旧，导致出现了有关可靠性问题以及装配和加工不良的广泛报道。最近，Unicomp投资购买了全新的模具，以在25年中制造出第一个全新的Model M，简称为New ModelM。
 我最近发现自己需要新的键盘。虽然Model M并不是我的第一个键盘，但它却是我在高中打字课上使用的键盘，从那以后，我就一直渴望那种那种轻快，弹力十足的打字快感。
 由于COVID-19大流行以及随后的停工对小型企业造成了灾难性的影响，我将新年做为一项新的决议，将为支持本地小型企业提供更多支持。 Unicomp工厂位于我家北部3个小时的地方，因此我认为，即使我对键盘不满意，但我至少会尽自己的一份力量来支持仍在该公司生产产品的少数计算机外围设备制造商之一。美国。
  我差点订购了带有Windows键和PC键盘的所有存货的New ModelM。毕竟，我并不太在乎键盘，因此，如果我的Command键上印有Saint Hannes十字勋章，Windows徽标或Linux企鹅，我也不会在意。无论如何，您都可以换下键帽，Unicomp出售各种键帽。 
我的挂断是沿着顶部行的Mac专用键，可让我控制亮度，音量，任务控制和其他功能。加上基本型号缺少Apple键盘上的功能键。当然，我可以重新编程以代替它，但是我决定使用Mac布局的键盘可以大大简化我的生活。
 我在12月中旬联系了Unicomp支持人员，以查看他们是否愿意为我建造一个与Spacesaver M布局相同的New ModelM。我知道Unicomp受到COVID-19的严重打击，从而延迟了即将推出的Mini M键盘原定于2020年3月，所以我没有寄予希望。但是，他们很快做出回应，告诉我他们可以在1月中旬制作一个自定义键盘，然后再次与他们联系。
 快闪到1月中旬，我再次与Unicomp支持联系，以了解他们是否可以构建我的自定义New ModelM。他们说可以，并提供了有关如何订购它的说明：
  结帐过程非常笨拙，定制和运输后的总成本为145.87美元。这很昂贵，但仅比目前市场上的许多高端机械键盘高。
 有人告诉我，组装和运输键盘可能需要最多两周的时间，但由于运输距离很近，它仅在三天内就交付了，并在第二天就到达了。
 我现在输入的键盘可能非常独特：Mac布局的New ModelM。
  New Model M的实用工业设计不会赢得任何时尚大奖。它具有简单的黑色底座以及白色和灰色按键组合。 Mac的变种非常裸露。它包括您在全尺寸Mac键盘中期望的所有键-命令，选项，功能，亮度等，但不要在键帽上看到Susan Kare设计的图标。诸如Command和Exposé之类的特殊键具有简单的文本标签，而亮度，音量和媒体键则具有通用的象形文字。 
就尺寸而言，New Model M是野兽。它宽17.9英寸（45.5厘米），深7.5英寸（19厘米），高1.96（5厘米）英寸，重3磅11盎司（约1.7千克）。数字键盘使键盘特别宽，因此，如果您将键盘和定位设备并排放置，可能会造成妨碍。我更喜欢将键盘放在膝盖上，所以这对我来说不是问题。
 New Model M可以与我的iMac一起使用。音量，亮度和媒体键效果很好，Exposé也是如此。我必须对Dashboard键进行编程，以在“系统偏好设置”＆gt;中触发启动板。键盘＆gt;快捷方式（有趣的是，仪表板键注册为F12）。
  奇怪的是，Unicomp用Eject替换了Insert键，直到我尝试在ThinkPad上键入内容并按DVD并弹出DVD托盘时，我才注意到这一点。尽管这是全尺寸Mac键盘的标准配置，但另一个奇怪之处是数字键盘上没有Num Lock键。而是由清除键代替。另外，我对手指枪的小象形文字在“删除”键上杀死字母“ a”感到很满意。
 如果您是在标准布局之间争论，还是在Mac布局上多花了20美元，我认为如果您要使用键盘的主计算机是Mac，这笔额外的钱是值得的。
  让我们谈谈真正重要的是什么：在New Model M上打字感觉如何？很难在文本中传达这种经验，但我会尽力而为。
 请务必注意，M型不是机械键盘！取而代之的是，它使用了一种弯曲弹簧机制，该机制比传统的机械键盘机制要古老得多。两者之间的主要区别在于弹簧的大小。在屈曲的弹簧键盘中，您按下一个键帽，该键帽将按下一个直接位于促动按键的水平面上的弹簧。在机械键盘中，一点点塑料就能操纵按键，而弹簧只需将按键返回到其原始位置即可。
 在屈曲弹簧机构中，弹簧是秀场上的明星。如果拔下其中一个键帽，您会看到弹簧固定在孔中。 
功能上的区别在于，翘曲键的弹簧具有某种“弹力”，而机械键则没有。很难传达，但是您键入时按键会发出轻微的“刺耳”的声音。虽然不是绝对安静，但声音不是特别大。我不担心孩子们在隔壁房间里醒来时醒来，它似乎比亚当·恩格斯特（Adam Engst）可靠的老式Das键盘安静，当我们在Slack通话时，这听起来像是在踩马。我录制了一段在New Model M上打字的视频，因此您可以感觉到声音。
  屈曲弹簧因需要大量的力来致动而闻名。我之前很担心，因为反复按下按钮时前臂肌肉会变得很紧张，这就是为什么我使用立式鼠标并且很少再在计算机上玩游戏的原因（请参阅“ Anker的立式鼠标可提供廉价的RSI缓解功能”，7 2018年12月）。但是，我对新模型M所需的启动力丝毫没有感到困扰。
 实际上，我发现按键非常轻巧。弹簧具有足够的抵抗力，因此您不会仅通过触摸键帽就可以意外激活键，但是一旦您决定按下该键，它就会在如此令人满意的咔嗒声之前移动。我估计实际的激活行程与Apple的Magic Keyboard大致相同。
 单击和按键触底之间的额外行程有助于缓冲手指。当我用Apple键盘打字时，有时指尖会不断地撞到键盘上，这使我感到痛苦。我对New Model M没有这种痛苦。
 当我在键盘之间时，我使用了超级便宜的沃尔玛键盘，它是如此糊状，就像在土豆泥上打字一样。这对我的前臂造成了压力，因为感觉就像我的手指在泥泞中工作。我不能说New Model M消除了我的前臂疼痛，但在我尝试过的所有键盘中，这种疼痛加重幅度最小。
  我喜欢用此键盘打字，但没有完美的选择。首先，虽然美学是主观的，但我发现新模型丑陋。但是，比起整体美观而言，更令人反感的是为Caps Lock和功能指示灯使用了明亮的蓝色LED。也许只是我的眼睛很敏感，但我发现蓝色LED太亮了，这些天它们在计算机组件中已完全被过度使用。
  New Model M是打字键盘，而不是游戏键盘。如果您关心“重影”，那么它不是适合您的键盘。重影是指键盘同时按下多个键时停止注册新的按键。您可以使用小指按住两个Shift键并输入以下命令，以在当前键盘上对此进行测试： 
这很糟糕，但同样，它主要涉及游戏玩家，他们可能必须一次按住多个键。 Apple Magic Keyboard的性能大致相同：
  延迟已久的Mini M（如果曾经发售）将具有我希望New Model M具有的一项功能：可拆卸的USB电缆。以我的经验，像这样的长期使用物品，首先要使用的是电缆（例如，我的2007年索尼MDR-V6耳机上的电缆），因此能够轻松更换它是一个很好的选择。
 关于新模型M的一些事情要比我在高中使用的旧模型M逊色。当我挤压键盘的两侧时，会有轻微的吱吱声。最初的M型女士坚如磐石，大概是因为它们是用石头雕刻而成的。但是，这在键入时没有任何区别。另一个降级是使用单件式键帽。较旧的Model M型号在每个按键上都有一个外壳，可以轻松打开和关闭，因此您可以更改按键的外观，而不必卸下整个按键，也不会弄乱弹簧机构。在新型号M上，您必须删除整个密钥。同样，这是一个无所作为。
 从长远来看，我可能遇到的最后一个小问题是Command键上的尖锐边缘。由于我的左手拇指的一侧经常碰到钥匙的角，因此边缘会刺激我的拇指。最好在Control，Option和Command键上倒角。
  如果我不喜欢New Model M，那么我至少可以安慰自己，因为我已经向一些小型的本地公司投入了一些钱。但是我喜欢它！虽然它不会赢得选美比赛或视频游戏比赛的胜利，但打字却是一种乐趣。我每天都在打字。
 有一种称为林迪效应的理论，该理论指出，不易腐烂的事物持续的时间越长，持续的时间就越长。例如，如果某本书已出版50年，则可以合理地假设它会再出版50年。通过这种测量，拥有37年历史的Model M确实是“ lindy”。这是永恒的设计，我很高兴Unicomp继承了这一传统。
 最后，我给您留下我小儿子的视频，帮助我录制键盘声音。
note_en:
1984 was a landmark year in computing. It was the debut year of the Macintosh, of course, but it also spawned another piece of timeless computer hardware: the IBM Model M keyboard, which Matt Neuburg argued is the greatest keyboard of all time (see “ The Greatest Computer Keyboard of All Time?,” 27 February 2009).
 The Model M represents a long-gone era of keyboards designed to satisfy typewriter users, featuring satisfyingly clicky buckling-spring keys. In 1992, IBM offloaded most of its keyboard manufacturing to Lexmark, which continued to produce Model M keyboards for Big Blue. The industry eventually moved to cheaper and mushier mechanisms like rubber domes, and Lexmark and IBM both bowed out of the Model M business in the late 1990s. However, a group of Lexmark employees bought the factory in Lexington, Kentucky and formed Unicomp.
 Clicky keyboards, once a niche item, have come back into fashion in recent years. They’re now so popular that mainstream companies offer mechanical “gaming” keyboards you can buy in many brick-and-mortar stores, even Walmart. Meanwhile, Unicomp has been churning out those classic Model M clicky keyboards since the days of Apple’s apparent doom.
 Unicomp even makes a Mac-specific model, the  Spacesaver M, but I’d be hesitant to recommend it. That’s because, over the years, the old Lexmark tooling has worn down, leading to widespread reports of reliability issues and poor fit and finish. Recently, Unicomp invested in brand-new tooling to make the first brand-new Model M in a quarter-century, simply named the  New Model M.
 I recently found myself in need of a new keyboard. While the Model M wasn’t my first keyboard, it was what I used in my high school typing class, and ever since I have longed for that clicky, bouncy, typing thrill.
 With the COVID-19 pandemic and subsequent lockdowns having a catastrophic impact on small businesses, I’ve made it something of a New Year’s resolution to do more to support small local businesses. The Unicomp factory is only 3 hours north of my house, so I figured even if I wasn’t totally happy with the keyboard, I’d at least be doing my part to support one of the few computer peripheral manufacturers still making products in the United States.
  I nearly ordered a stock New Model M with Windows keys and all the trappings of a PC keyboard. After all, I don’t look at my keyboard all that much, so I don’t care that much if my Command key is emblazoned with the  Saint Hannes cross, a Windows logo, or a Linux penguin. Regardless, you can swap out the keycaps, and Unicomp  sells a variety of keycaps.
 My hangup was the Mac-specific keys along the top row that let me control brightness, volume, Mission Control, and other functions. Plus the base model lacked the Function key seen on Apple keyboards. Sure, I could reprogram something to take its place, but I decided that it would make my life substantially easier to have a keyboard with a Mac layout.
 I contacted Unicomp support in mid-December to see if they would be willing to build me a New Model M with the same layout as the Spacesaver M. I knew Unicomp had been hit especially hard by COVID-19, delaying the upcoming  Mini M keyboard that was due in March 2020, so I didn’t get my hopes up. However, they responded quickly, telling me that they could do a custom keyboard in mid-January and to contact them again then.
 Flash forward to mid-January, and I got in touch with Unicomp support again to see if they could build my custom New Model M. They said yes, and gave me instructions on how to order it:
  The checkout process was pretty clunky, and the total cost after customizations and shipping was $145.87. That’s pricey, but no more so than many high-end mechanical keyboards currently on the market.
 I was told it might take up to two weeks to build and ship my keyboard, but it shipped in just three days and arrived the day after it was shipped, likely due to my close proximity.
 The keyboard I’m now typing on may very well be unique: a New Model M with a Mac layout.
  The New Model M’s utilitarian industrial design won’t win any style awards. It features a simple black base, with a mix of white and gray keys. The Mac variant is pretty bare bones. It includes all the keys you would expect in a full-size Mac keyboard— Command, Option, Function, brightness, etc.—but don’t expect Susan Kare-designed icons on the keycaps. Special keys like Command and Exposé have simple text labels, while brightness, volume, and media keys have generic pictographs.
  In terms of size, the New Model M is a beast. It’s 17.9 inches (45.5 cm) wide, 7.5 inches (19 cm) deep, and 1.96 (5 cm) inches high, and weighs 3 pounds 11 ounces (about 1.7 kg). The number pad makes the keyboard especially wide, so it might get in the way if you keep your keyboard and pointing device side-by-side. I prefer to put the keyboard in my lap, so that’s not an issue for me.
 The New Model M worked with my iMac out of the box. Volume, brightness, and media keys worked perfectly, as did Exposé. I had to program the Dashboard key to trigger Launchpad in System Preferences > Keyboard > Shortcuts (interestingly, the Dashboard key registers as F12).
  One odd thing is that Unicomp replaced the Insert key with Eject, something I didn’t notice until I tried to press Insert while typing on my ThinkPad and the DVD tray popped out. Another oddity, though it’s standard on full-sized Mac keyboards, is that there is no Num Lock key on the number pad. Instead, it’s replaced by a Clear key. Also, I’m amused by the little pictograph of finger guns slaying the letter “a” on the Delete key.
 If you’re debating between the standard layout or dropping an extra $20 on the Mac layout, I think the extra money is worth it if the main computer you’ll be using the keyboard with is a Mac.
  Let’s talk about what’s really important: what is it like to type on the New Model M? It’s difficult to convey such an experience in text, but I’ll do my best.
 It’s important to note that the Model M is  not a mechanical keyboard! Instead, it uses a buckling spring mechanism, which is a much older design than the traditional mechanical keyboard mechanism. The chief difference between the two is the size of the spring. In a buckling spring keyboard, you press a keycap, which presses a spring that sits directly on a level that actuates the keypress. In a mechanical keyboard, a little bit of plastic actuates the keypress, with the spring simply returning the key to its original position.
 In a buckling spring mechanism, the spring is the star of the show. If you pull off one of the keycaps you can see the spring sitting in its well.
  The functional difference is that a buckling key’s springs have a certain “springiness” that mechanical keys do not. It’s hard to convey, but the keys make a slight “sproing” sound as you type. It’s not particularly loud, though it’s definitely not silent. I don’t worry about waking up the kids when they’re sleeping in the next room, and it seems quieter than Adam Engst’s old reliable Das Keyboard, which sounds like horses stampeding when we’re on Slack calls. I made a  video of me typing on the New Model M so you can get a sense of the sound.
  Buckling springs have a reputation for taking a great amount of force to actuate. I was worried about that up front, because my forearm muscles tend to get painfully tense when pressing buttons repeatedly, which is why I use a vertical mouse and rarely play games on computers anymore (see “ Anker’s Vertical Mouse Provides Cheap RSI Relief,” 7 December 2018). However, I have not been bothered at all by the activation force necessary for the New Model M.
 In fact, I find the keys to be quite light to press. The spring offers enough resistance so you won’t accidentally activate a key just by touching its keycap, but once you decide to press that key, it moves ever so slightly before that ever-so-satisfying click. I estimate the actual activation travel to be about the same as Apple’s Magic Keyboard.
 The additional travel between the click and the key bottoming out helps cushion your fingers. When I typed with Apple keyboards, I sometimes experienced pain in my fingertips from where they were constantly smashing into the keyboard. I have no such pain with the New Model M.
 When I was between keyboards, I used a super-cheap Walmart keyboard that was so mushy it was like typing on mashed potatoes. It was a strain on my forearms because it felt like my fingers were working in mud. I can’t say the New Model M has eliminated my forearm pain, but it aggravates it the least of all the keyboards I’ve tried.
  I like typing with this keyboard, but nothing’s perfect. For one, while aesthetics are subjective, I find the New Model M ugly. But perhaps more offensive than the overall aesthetic is the use of bright blue LEDs for the Caps Lock and Function indicator lights. Maybe it’s just my highly sensitive eyes, but I find blue LEDs to be entirely too bright, and they’re totally overused in computer components these days.
  The New Model M is a typing keyboard, not a gaming keyboard. If you care about “ ghosting,” it’s not the keyboard for you. Ghosting is when a keyboard stops registering new key presses when you hold down multiple keys at the same time. You can test this on your current keyboard by holding down both Shift keys with your pinkies and typing:
    That’s pretty bad, but again, it primarily concerns gamers, who might have to hold down several keys at once. The Apple Magic Keyboard performs about the same:
  The long-delayed Mini M, if it ever ships, will sport one feature I wish the New Model M had: a detachable USB cable. In my experience, the first thing to go on long-term use items like this is the cable (like on my Sony MDR-V6 headphones from 2007), so it’d be good to be able to replace it easily.
 There are a few things about the New Model M that are inferior to the old Model Ms I used in high school. There is a slight creak when I squeeze either side of the keyboard. The original Model Ms were rock solid, presumably because they had been carved from stone. However, this doesn’t make any difference when typing. Another downgrade is the use of single-piece keycaps. The old Model M models had an external shell over each key that easily popped on and off, so you could change a key’s look without removing the entire key and possibly messing up the spring mechanism. On the New Model M, you have to remove the entire key. Again, this is a nitpick.
 A final small issue I have that may become larger in the long run is the sharp edges on the Command key. Since the side of my left thumb often rides against the corner of that key, the edge irritates my thumb. It’d be nice to have chamfered edges on the Control, Option, and Command keys.
  If I didn’t love the New Model M, I could at least console myself with the fact that I had thrown some money to a small, somewhat local business. But I do love it! While it won’t win beauty contests or video game tournaments, it’s a joy to type with. And I’m typing all day, every day.
 There is a theory called the  Lindy effect, which states that the longer a non-perishable thing lasts, the longer it is likely to last. For example, if a book has been in publication for 50 years, it’s reasonable to assume that it will be in publication for another 50 years. By that measure, the 37-year-old Model M is indeed “lindy.” It’s a timeless design, and I’m glad Unicomp is carrying on the tradition.
 Finally, I’ll leave you a  video of my youngest son helping me record keyboard sounds.
title: 新型M是美国制造的键盘，可为您的打字增添弹性
title_en: New Model M Is an American-Made Keyboard That Puts a Spring Back in Your Typing

我不知道这次发生了什么，但这使我疯狂了一个早晨……让我们开始讲一个故事，说那些ARM Mac电脑是美丽/幻想/伟大的产品！
 因此，我买了一台MacBook Air，然后我开始想：“不要懒惰地重新准备安装的所有东西，自从Snow Leopard以来，我还没有做，而是要克隆它……”所以，让我们与往常一样，尝试使用很棒且不可或缺的抄送克隆器。因此，我按住电源按钮启动了MBA，进入恢复模式，将Mac设置为“共享驱动器”启动（是的，CMD + R不适用于ARM Mac），并且将Thunderbolt 3电缆与我的2019 iMac和…连接在一起。没有。 MBA磁盘没有出现在我的桌面上。
 因此，我进行了一些研究，发现通过这种新的“共享驱动器模式”，SSD不再被视为“存储驱动器”（挂载为/ dev / diskN），而是成为了网络共享驱动器（使用SMB协议） ）😳
 这对我来说毫无意义，而且我已经开始思考不可能克隆iMac SSD，实际上是因为CCC只能将其视为网络驱动器，并且无法从Intel克隆ARM安装或从Intel克隆ARM安装。安装。
 好的，没问题，我将使用迁移助手，因为我想使一切恢复正常，这是我从恢复启动和＆gt;磁盘实用程序＆gt;删除Macintosh SSD以便从干净的OS启动。
  所有的混乱都从这里开始，当我尝试重新安装Big Sur时出现了一个奇怪的错误：
 这意味着Big Sur无法看到驱动器内部的用户，无法将身份验证授予磁盘，而且自从我在十秒钟前对其进行格式化以来，这似乎很明显！ 
好的，不用担心，我尝试从iMac CCC备份中重新安装用户
  是的，这并不像我所怀疑的那样……因此，让我们在线检查一下，Apple建议在终端上键入“ resetpassword”来进行一个长程序，但要一个简短的过程，这是一个机会，但是由于它没有找到用户，因此没有可重置的密码。
  嗯...好吧，让我们看看磁盘实用程序里面发生了什么：
  嗯，这个SSD看起来对我来说没有擦除！让我们再试一次：
    决不！啊，现在呢？我无法安装该系统，因为“磁盘工具”尚未擦除驱动器，但它仅告诉SSD控制器这些空间可以被覆盖，但尚未被覆盖！因此，macOS安装程序会找到一个没有用户或类似内容的系统目录。苹果这真是个愚蠢的行为！在用户这样做之前发出警报！
 在线搜索后，我在Reddit上发现了一个长线程，是否有某些用户由于此错误而将M1 Macs退回给Apple！
  M1机械师在还原方面存在一个已知问题。基本上，它不起作用。我真的很惊讶，Apple支持部门没有通知您此问题，至少没有确保您有能力使用Apple Configurator使用另一台Mac还原它 
我无法重新安装Apple并通过另一台带有Apple Configurator的Mac来完成所有建议。
   [-bash-3.2＃diskutil deleteDisk APFS无标题/ dev / disk3s3指定了卷而不是整个磁盘：/ dev / disk3s3指定整个磁盘，或者使用diskutil deleteVolume擦除特定分区上的卷
  [-bash-3.2＃diskutil deleteVolume APFS无标题/ dev / disk3s3开始擦除准备擦除APFS卷内容检查安装状态通过删除并重新添加以删除APFS卷disk3s3，从其APFS容器中删除APFS卷卸载disk3s3擦除被引用的ART会话6CC401CB-3079–4C36–986A-DA03F6495762删除卷删除任何预引导和恢复目录准备将APFS卷添加到APFS容器disk3创建APFS卷创建新的APFS卷disk3s3设置安装状态设置卷权限完成擦除-bash-3.2＃
 哦，这太不可思议了，这不是Disk Utility第一次无法运行，而是来自CLI的diskutil可以运行。

 所以现在磁盘应该全部清除/擦除/像新的一样有光泽，aaaanndddd，我们开始了！最后！

note_en:
I don’t know what went on with this time but it has drove me crazy for one morning… let’s start the story saying that those ARM Macs are beautiful/fantistic/great products!
 So I bought a MacBook Air, and in order to start I thought “t oo lazy to prepare again all the thing of my installation, I haven’t done it, and I’m cloning it, since Snow Leopard…” so, let’s try as usual with the great and indispensable  Carbon Copy Cloner. So I started the MBA holding the power button, entered recovery mode, set the mac to boot as “Shared Drive” (yes CMD+R doesn’t work with ARM Macs) with a connected thunderbolt 3 cable with my 2019 iMac and…. nothing. The MBA disk didn’t appear on my desktop.
 So I’ve made a little research and found that with this new “Shared Drive mode” the SSD is no more seen as a “storage drive” (mounted as /dev/diskN) but it became a network shared drive (with SMB protocol) 😳
 This make no sense to me, and I already started thinking that it was not possible to clone the iMac SSD, infact it was, because CCC saw it only as a network drive and is not possible to clone an ARM installation with/from an Intel installation.
 Okay, no problem, I’ll use migration assistant, since I wanted to make the things clean I started in recovery boot and > Disk Utility > Erase Macintosh SSD in order to start from a clean OS.
  All the mess is started here, when I tried to reinstall Big Sur a weird error appeared:
 This means that Big Sur can’t see users inside the drive, to garant the authentication to the disk, and it seems pretty obvious since I formatted it ten seconds ago!
  Okay… no panic, I tried to reinstall the user from my iMac CCC backup
  Nope, this don’t work as I suspected… so, let’s check online, Apple suggests  a long procedure and a short by typing “resetpassword” in terminal, that was a chance but since it founds no users, there’s no passwords to reset.
  hmm… okay, let’s see inside the disk utility what is going on there:
  uhm, this SSD doesn’t look erased to me! Let’s try again:
    No way! Argh, and now? I can’t install the system because Disk Utility has not erased the drive but it has only told to the SSD controller that those space can be overwritten but it has not overwritten it! So the macOS installer founds a system directory with no users or something similar. That’s a pretty stupid behavior Apple! Put an alert before the users do this!
 Searched online and I found a long thread on Reddit, were some users was sendig back the M1 Macs to Apple due this error!
  M1 machiens have a known issue with Restoring. Basically, it doesn’t work. I’m really surprised Apple Support didn’t inform you of this issue, at least without making sure you had the means to restore it using another Mac with Apple Configurator
 No way I reinstall and do all the thing Apple suggests with another Mac with Apple Configurator.
   [-bash-3.2# diskutil eraseDisk APFS Untitled /dev/disk3s3 A volume was specified instead of a whole disk: /dev/disk3s3 Specify a whole disk, or instead use diskutil eraseVolume to erase a volume on a specific partition
  [-bash-3.2# diskutil eraseVolume APFS Untitled /dev/disk3s3 Started erase Preparing to erase APFS Volume content Checking mount state Erasing APFS Volume disk3s3 by deleting and re-adding Deleting APFS Volume from its APFS Container Unmounting disk3s3 Erasing any ART session referenced by 6CC401CB-3079–4C36–986A-DA03F6495762 Deleting Volume Removing any Preboot and Recovery Directories Preparing to add APFS Volume to APFS Container disk3 Creating APFS Volume Created new APFS Volume disk3s3 Setting mount state Setting volume permissions Finished erase -bash-3.2#
 Oh…that’s incredible, is not the first time that Disk Utility is not working, instead diskutil from CLI works.
   WTF? Three partitions? I’ve just erased this disk. Hmm… let’s try one
    Oh! This time worked and has deleted the Data volume (that was the trouble because the Big Sur installer was stupidly looking for the user there)!
 Sooo now the disk should be all clear/erased/as shiny as new, aaaanndddd here we go! Finally!
  Because Apple doesn’t put a warning alerting that it doesn’t really format a drive and Big Sur installer is stupid and it doesn’t see it as a “clean” drive but it see the “Data” Volume and it tries to find a user to authorize the installation, but since the drive has no users, it fails.

全球有超过17亿资金不足的人，其中大部分来自新兴市场。对于他们来说，获得贷款可能很困难，这是金融科技公司试图解决的问题。他们做到这一点的一种方法是通过专有算法通过承销信贷来促进金融包容性。
 FairMoney就是这样的公司之一，它自称为“新兴市场的移动银行革命”。 FairMoney由Laurin Hainy，Matthieu Gendreau和Nicolas Berthozat创立，是一家获得许可的在线贷方，可为新兴市场中服务欠缺的消费者提供即时贷款和账单支付。
 在尼日利亚推出移动借贷服务三年后，该公司于2020年8月在亚洲第二大人口大国印度开设了商店。
  在扩张之前，FairMoney在尼日利亚的贷款支出经历了指数级增长。去年，它支付的贷款总额为9300万美元，比2019年增长128％，比2018年运营的第一年惊人地达到3,189％的增长率。目前，该公司预计将支付1.4亿美元的贷款到2021年底的交易量。
 该公司首席执行官Hainy对TechCrunch表示：“我认为我们能够比某些竞争对手多支出25-30％，而且我认为我们是市场领导者。”但是与传统银行相比，它是该地区第七大数字金融服务提供商。
  自2017年在尼日利亚推出以来，FairMoney已走了很长一段路。在运营的第一年，该公司的用户数量略超过100,000。现在，它声称拥有130万唯一用户，他们已经提出了超过650万笔贷款申请。 FairMoney提供的贷款从1,500韩元（3.30美元）到500,000韩元（1,110.00美元），最长的贷款额度为12个月。 Hainy说，年百分比率在30％到260％之间-高APR，这是由于尼日利亚较高的违约率。也就是说，FairMoney还声称不良贷款率低于10％。
 这位首席执行官表示，以数据为依据的见解是其选择向印度扩张的原因。印度市场与尼日利亚的市场非常相似。在亚洲国家，只有36％的成年人可以获得信贷，这使大约1.41亿人的小额信贷银行无法使用，市场尚未开发。但是与尼日利亚不同，印度在贷款业务方面拥有更好的单位经济学，并且拥有更加友好的监管环境。 
Hainy说：“如果我们的志向是为新兴市场建立领先的移动银行，那么我们需要从非常大的市场开始。” “我们在10个不同的市场上对我们的产品进行了测试，以检验诸如收益经济学，不良贷款，风险成本，客户获取成本，基础设施成本和印度等方面的问题。”
  在六个月前进行扩张之后，FairMoney声称已经处理了来自100,000多个唯一用户的逾半百万贷款申请。这个数字每天下降到5,000至6,000美元的贷款申请，实际年利率为12-36％。 Hainy说，该公司通过零广告支出或营销来实现这一目标。
 由于国际扩张背后的艰巨后勤工作，这家总部位于非洲的初创企业要在非洲大陆以外扩张，将面临挑战。尽管很少见，但是有一些初创公司已经承担了这样的任务。去年，拥有1500万用户和24,000多名代理商的尼日利亚金融科技Paga收购了埃塞俄比亚软件公司Apposit，以快速将其扩展到埃塞俄比亚和墨西哥。
 FairMoney也走类似的道路。该公司希望在印度，法国和拉脱维亚拥有100多名员工，希望建立一支工程和营销团队。
 上个月，它聘请了Rohan Khara担任该公司的首席产品官（CPO），并协助其扩展业务。 Khara曾是印尼超级应用Gojek金融服务产品的负责人，并曾在Microsoft，Quikr和MobiKwik担任高级职务。 Hainy说，凭借Khara在印度和印度尼西亚等大型新兴市场中构建消费产品的丰富经验，FairMoney有望在尼日利亚和印度实现大规模增长。
 “我们都有共同的愿景，即新兴市场的金融服务需要修复，而对于我们而言，罗汉（Rohan）带来了专业知识，以使FairMoney的规模从近100万用户扩大到10或2000万用户。”
  Hainy生于德国，父亲是尼日利亚人，母亲是德国人，2015年，他在瑞典成立了一家送餐公司，开始了他的创业之旅。七个月后，他创立了Le Studio VC，这是一家总部位于巴黎的初创工作室，他以首席执行官身份运营了1500万欧元，为期三年。 
“三年后，我意识到成为投资人还不适合我。我觉得我还太年轻，我想自己做点什么，”他说。
 像英国的Revolut和德国的N26这样的Neobanks在整个欧洲都在兴起。 Hainy在注意到有多少人在访问期间无法获得负担得起的金融服务之后，想为尼日利亚创建这样的组织。
 但是，尽管研究了其他Neobank模型，但Hainy和他的团队无法在尼日利亚这样的发展中市场复制它们。由于采用严格的分配贷款方法，尼日利亚的银行仍然严重缺乏信贷服务。意识到机遇，他们利用信贷第一模式推出了FairMoney，作为一家新银行。像巴西的努班克银行一样，FairMoney开始提供贷款来解决信贷问题。但其更广阔的视野不只是成为一家数字银行，还不仅仅是一家商业银行。
 该公司正在努力争取小额信贷银行的执照，以便在尼日利亚以小额信贷银行的身份运营。但是，根据首席执行官的说法，商业银行牌照将需要更长的时间，可能需要五到十年。
 “在未来的五到十年中，我想认为尼日利亚五家最大的商业银行中有两家将是新银行。我们希望FairMoney成为其中之一，”他说。
 这家总部位于拉各斯和巴黎的公司在2019年筹集了1100万美元的A轮融资。从现在起到获得商业银行牌照的这段时间里，Hainy表示，该公司将提高其B轮融资以将自己定位于该任务。
  在印度之后，FairMoney将扩展到哪个新兴市场？首席执行官说，目前看不到任何东西。该公司计划从信贷主导的价值主张转变为一家完整的金融服务提供商，加深其垂直市场，并暂时复制尼日利亚在印度的增长。
note_en:
There are over  1.7 billion underbanked people globally, the majority of which are from emerging markets. For them, accessing loans can be difficult, which is a problem fintechs try to solve. One way they do this is by promoting financial inclusion by underwriting credit via a proprietary algorithm.
 One such company is  FairMoney, which describes itself as “the mobile banking revolution for emerging markets.” FairMoney, founded by  Laurin Hainy,  Matthieu Gendreau and  Nicolas Berthozat, is a licensed online lender that provides instant loans and bill payments to underserved consumers in emerging markets.
 Three years after launching  its mobile lending service in Nigeria,  the company   set up shop in India , Asia’s second-most populous country in August 2020.
  Before expanding, FairMoney experienced exponential growth in Nigeria in terms of loans disbursement. La st year,  it disbursed a total loan volume of $93 million, representing a 128% increase from 2019 and a staggering 3,189% growth rate from its first year of operation in 2018. As it stands, the company is projecting a $140 million loan disbursement volume by the end of 2021.
 “I think we’ve been able to disburse 25-30% more than some of our competitors and I think we’re a market leader,” Hainy, the company’s CEO told TechCrunch. But compared with traditional banks, it was the seventh-largest digital financial services provider in that area.
  FairMoney has come a long way since its Nigeria launch in 2017. In its first year of operation, the company had little over 100,000 users. Now, it claims to have 1.3 million unique users who have made over 6.5 million loan applications.  FairMoney offers loans from ₦1,500 ($3.30) to ₦500,000 ($1,110.00) with its longest loan facility standing at 12 months. Annual percentage rates fall within 30% to 260% — the high APR, Hainy says, is due to higher default rates in Nigeria. That said, FairMoney also claims to have an NPL ratio lower than 10%.
 According to the CEO, data-driven insights was behind the choice to expand to India. The Indian market is quite similar to Nigeria’s. In the Asian country, only 36% of adults have access to credit, leaving an untapped market of about 141 million people microfinance banks do not serve. But unlike Nigeria, India has better unit economics for the lending business and a more friendly regulatory environment.
 “If our ambition is to build the leading mobile bank for emerging markets, we need to start with very large markets,” Hainy said. “We tested our products in 10 different markets checking out for things like what the yield economics is like, NPLs, cost of risk, customer acquisition cost, cost of infrastructure and India stood out to us.”
  Following its expansion six months ago, FairMoney claims to have processed more than half a million loan applications from over 100,000 unique users. This number trickles down to $5,000 to $6,000 loan applications per day with APR standing at 12-36%. Hainy says the company has achieved this with zero ad spend or marketing.
 Due to the daunting logistics behind international expansions, it’s challenging for an African-based startup to expand outside the shores of the continent. Although a rarity, there are a couple of startups to have undertaken such a task. Last year, Nigerian fintech Paga with 15 million users and a network of over 24,000 agents acquired Ethiopian software company Apposit to  fast-track its expansion into Ethiopia and Mexico.
 FairMoney is on a similar path, as well. And with over 100 staff spread across Nigeria, France, and Latvia, the company hopes to build an engineering and marketing team in India.
 Last month, it hired the services of  Rohan Khara to become its chief product officer (CPO) and facilitate the expansion. Khara was the former head of product for financial services for Indonesian super app Gojek and held senior roles at Microsoft, Quikr and MobiKwik. Hainy says with Khara’s wealth of experience building consumer products in large emerging markets — India and Indonesia — FairMoney is poised for massive growth in Nigeria and India.
 “We both share the vision that financial services in emerging markets need fixing and for us, Rohan brings the expertise to see FairMoney scale from almost a million users to 10 or 20 million users.”
  Born in Germany to a Nigerian father and German mother, Hainy began his entrepreneurial journey in 2015 by launching a food delivery company in Sweden. Seven months later, he founded Le Studio VC, a Paris-based startup studio and €15 million fund he ran as CEO for three years.
 “After those three years, I realised that being an investor wasn’t for me yet. I felt I was too young and I wanted to build something myself,” he said.
 Neobanks like Revolut in the UK and N26 in Germany were picking up across Europe. Hainy wanted to create such for Nigeria after noticing how much people lacked access to affordable financial services during a visit.
 But despite studying other neobank models, Hainy and his team couldn’t replicate them in a developing market like Nigeria. Credit was still significantly underserved by Nigerian banks because of the strict methodology employed in allocating loans. Sensing an opportunity, they launched FairMoney as a neobank by leveraging a credit-first model. Like Nubank in Brazil, FairMoney started off offering loans to solve the access to credit problem. But its broader vision is not to be just a digital bank but also a commercial bank.
 The company is working towards getting a microfinance bank license to operate as the former in Nigeria. However, according to the CEO, the commercial bank license will take longer maybe five to ten years.
 “In the next five to ten years, I’d like to think two out of the five largest commercial banks in Nigeria will be neobanks. We want FairMoney to be one of them,” he said.
 The Lagos and Paris-based company raised $11 million Series A in 2019. Between now and the time it will get a commercial bank license, Hainy says the company would’ve raised its Series B round to position itself for that task.
  After India, which emerging market will FairMoney expand to next? There’s none in sight at the moment, the CEO says. The company plans to move from a credit-led value proposition to a full financial service provider, deepen its verticals, and replicate Nigeria’s growth in India for now.
title: 拥有超过130万用户的尼日利亚金融科技FairMoney希望复制印度的增长
title_en: With over 1.3 million users, Nigerian-based fintech FairMoney wants to replicate growth in India

note:
DEF的背景故事是一个有趣的故事，讲述了过去15年来欧洲发生的事情，当时科技起步，欧洲人从硅谷返回。
 Cem Sertoglu于2005年从SelectMinds（他是创始人兼首席执行官）退出后，搬回土耳其。尽管他说自己“偶然成为了那里的第一位天使投资人”，但他显然是在正确的时间，正确的位置上合适的人。他告诉我：“我很幸运，最终在土耳其的一些首批重大成果中写了第一张支票。”
  2013年，Sertoglu与Evren Ucok（Peak Games和Trendyol的第一位天使）和Roland Manger（Earlybird）合作。罗马尼亚投资者丹·卢普（Dan Lupu）曾在英特尔投资公司（Intel Capital）负责该地区的业务，并加入了他们的行列。他们共同筹集了2014年设立的1.5亿美元“早鸟数字东方基金I”，重点是中东欧和土耳其。这曾经是一个可以找到高质量风险投资的领域，但是在风险投资方面却很少。
 此后，在2014年至2019年之间，该基金投资了UiPath，Hazelcast和Obilet。 UiPath已成为“机器人过程自动化（RPA）”领域的全球领导者。 Hazelcast是一家具有土耳其血统的低延迟数据处理平台创业公司。 Obilet是专注于庞大的土耳其城市间巴士旅行市场的市场。 DEF还退出了Vivense，Dolap和EMbonds，最近，该基金也退出了“土耳其之路” Vivense，成为当地顶级私募股权基金Actera。
 团队取得了令人瞩目的早期成功。 Peak Games，Trendyol，YemekSepeti和GittiGidiyor是迄今为止土耳其最大的四个技术出口。 Digital East Fund是所有这些公司的投资者。仅去年一年，Peak游戏就以18亿美元的现金退出了Zynga。
 截至2020年第四季度，该基金的指标为：投资倍数：24.9倍总内部收益率：104.4％净内部收益率：84.1％
   他告诉我，DEF在1.5亿美元的基金中已达到17倍的投资倍数。他认为：“这可能是欧洲有史以来最大的风投基金业绩，它不是来自柏林，不是来自伦敦，而是来自东欧。我们的一些合伙人告诉我们，他们认为我们是2014年全球顶级的风投基金，没有人能比这更好。 
高峰游戏原来是一个了不起的故事。从宏观经济角度看，土耳其经历了多么艰难。一家拥有100名员工的公司实际上以18亿美元的现金出售，这一事实…………对于这里的当地市场来说是惊人的。”
 DEF从土耳其的崛起，以及与柏林一家基金的关系，并不是风险投资基金最明显的道路。
 “我们早期意识到的一件事是，我们可以用自有资金进行投资并向我们的朋友组织辛迪加，但是要获得后续资金，我们总是必须走向全球。这使我们感到脆弱。这使我们感到我们始终依赖于他人对我们所面临机会的理解。因此，这是第一个基金创意出现的时候，” Sertoglu说。
 “我们认为东欧的机会与资本之间存在这种不寻常的错位。我们的第一只基金是1.5亿美元，与西方市场相比，这是非常古朴的规模。但是我们成为了该地区最大的基金，因此决定专注于A轮融资缺口，我们认为这是一个巨大的机会，因为我们认为A轮融资在很大程度上仍是本地公司。”
 “成为了解该地区的本地参与者将是一个优势，因此事实证明这是事实。在过去的八年中，我们基本上可以看到东欧的一切。幸运的是，我们找到了最大的一个，就是UiPath。我认为全世界很少有基金可以说，他们看到了任务授权所能带来的大部分机会，即使不是全部，”他说。
 “我们有支持本地冠军和全球市场竞争者的双重战略。 20年前，您必须在硅谷。现在，Transferwise来自爱沙尼亚，UiPath来自罗马尼亚。那甚至早在大流行之前。”
 Sertoglu总结道：“因此，我们现在有了新鲜的资金，紧随我们热衷于部署的非常成功的第一只基金之后。我们正在利用一切机会，看到有雄心勃勃的强大团队涌出该地区。我们有2亿欧元专注于该地区的这类机会。”
note_en:
The back-story to DEF is a fascinating tale of what happened to Europe in the last 15 years, as tech took off and Europeans returned from Silicon Valley.
 Following his exit from SelectMinds (where he was the Founder & CEO) in 2005,  Cem Sertoglu moved back to Turkey. Although he says he “accidentally became the first angel investor” there, he was clearly the right man, in the right place, at the right time. He told me: “I was very lucky and ended up writing the first checks in some of the first large outcomes in Turkey.”
  In 2013, Sertoglu partnered with Evren Ucok (the first angel in Peak Games and Trendyol), and Roland Manger (Earlybird). Dan Lupu, a Romanian investor who had covered the region for Intel Capital, joined them, and together they raised the ‘Earlybird Digital East Fund I’ set at $150m fund in 2014, focusing on CEE and Turkey. This was and is an area where there can be high-quality ventures to be found, but very little in the way of VC.
 Thereafter, between 2014 and 2019, the fund invested in UiPath, Hazelcast, and Obilet. UiPath has become a global leader in the area known as ‘Robotic Process Automation (RPA). Hazelcast is a low latency data processing platform startup with Turkish roots. Obilet is a marketplace focused for the massive Turkish intercity bus travel market. DEF has also exited Vivense, Dolap, and EMbonds and in more recent times the fund has exited Vivense, the “Wayfair of Turkey” to Actera, the top local PE fund.
 The team had spectacular early success. Peak Games, Trendyol, YemekSepeti and GittiGidiyor are the four largest Turkish tech exits to date. Digital East Fund was an investor in all of them. Peak games exited for $1.8 billion in cash to Zynga only last year.
 As of Q4 2020, the fund’s metrics are: Investment Multiple: 24.9x Gross IRR: 104.4% Net IRR: 84.1%
   He told me DEF has achieved a 17 times investment multiple on a $150 million fund. He thinks “this might be the biggest European VC fund performance in history, and it’s not coming from Berlin, it’s not coming from London, but it’s coming from Eastern Europe. We have been told by some of our LPs that they think we’re the top 2014 vintage VC fund in the world, nobody’s seen stronger numbers than this.”
 “Peak Games turned out to be a phenomenal story. When you look at how tough it’s been for Turkey, macroeconomically. The fact that a single company with 100 people essentially sold for $1.8 billion in cash, was just… it was staggering for the local market here.”
 DEF’s emergence from Turkey, together with its relationship with a fund in Berlin, was not the most obvious path for the VC fund.
 “One thing we realized early one was that we could invest with our own capital and syndicating to our friends, but for follow-on funding, we’d always have to go global. And that made us feel vulnerable. It made us feel we were always dependent on others’ comprehension of the opportunity that we were facing. So that’s when the first fund idea came out this was,” said Sertoglu.
 “We felt that there was this unusual dislocation between opportunity and capital in Eastern Europe. Our first fund was $150 million funds – I mean, a very quaint size compared to Western markets. But we became the largest fund in the region, and decided to focus on this series A gap where we felt that there was this big opportunity, because of the way we think series A is still very much a local play.”
 “Being a local player that understands the region would be an advantage, so this was proven to be true. We could essentially see pretty much everything in Eastern Europe for the last eight years. And we caught the biggest one, fortunately, which was UiPath. I think very few funds around the world can say that they see the majority if not all of the opportunities that fall into their mandate,” he said.
 “We have this dual strategy of backing local champions as well as contenders for global markets as well. 20 years ago you had to be in Silicon Valley. Now, Transferwise comes out of Estonia, UiPath comes out of Romania. And that was even before the pandemic.”
 Sertoglu concluded: “So we now have fresh capital, coming on the heels of a very successful first fund, which we’re keen to deploy. We’re calling all the opportunities, seeing very ambitious, strong teams coming out of the region. And we have 200 million euros to focus on these types of opportunities in the region.”

纽约（路透社）-纽约总检察长周三提起诉讼，要求关闭加密货币平台Coinseed Inc，理由是该公司涉嫌欺诈数千名投资者，包括收取隐性交易费和出售“毫无价值的”数字代币。
  总检察长莱蒂蒂亚·詹姆斯（Letitia James）说，Coinseed在未注册为经纪交易商的情况下交易了比特币等加密货币，并未经授权就出售了“ CSD”代币，以为其移动应用程序初创公司筹集资金。
 詹姆斯还起诉了Coinseed首席执行官Delgerdalai Davaasambuu和前首席财务官Sukhbat Lkhagvadorj，称他们夸大了位于曼哈顿中城的公司的管理经验，而Lkhagvadorj则将自己歪曲为前华尔街商人。
 根据詹姆斯的说法，Coinseed的欺诈行为总计超过100万美元，詹姆斯还在寻求为投资者提供赔偿。
 美国证券交易委员会针对代币对Coinseed和Davaasambuu提起了相关诉讼，两家监管机构均称其于2017年12月至2018年5月之间被出售。
  达瓦萨姆布乌（Davaasambuu）在一封电子邮件中指詹姆斯的诉讼说：“我100％肯定诉讼中有虚假指控。” “他们怪怪初创公司运作的本质是很奇怪的。”
  詹姆斯说，两名被告都在纽约长岛市居住，但达瓦萨姆布乌表示有意返回他的祖国蒙古。 
詹姆斯在一份声明中说：“不论大小，不受监管和欺诈的虚拟货币实体在纽约将不再被容忍。”  总检察长表示，Coinseed曾将CSD令牌吹捧为``对于希望在加密货币市场上赚钱的年轻人来说是一个绝佳的机会''，并且移动应用程序得到了“保证”以帮助将其市场价值推高。  她的诉讼说：“实际上，近三年后，CSD令牌尚未在任何地方列出。”  詹姆斯在曼哈顿最高法院提起诉讼，指控其违反了纽约州证券法《马丁法》。 SEC的诉讼正在曼哈顿联邦法院进行。
note_en:
NEW YORK (Reuters) - New York’s attorney general filed a lawsuit on Wednesday to shut down the cryptocurrency platform Coinseed Inc for allegedly defrauding thousands of investors, including by charging hidden trading fees and selling “worthless” digital tokens.
  Attorney General Letitia James said Coinseed traded cryptocurrencies such as bitcoin without registering as a broker-dealer, and sold “CSD” tokens without authorization to raise money for its mobile application startup.
 James also sued Coinseed Chief Executive Delgerdalai Davaasambuu and former Chief Financial Officer Sukhbat Lkhagvadorj, saying they overstated the midtown Manhattan-based company’s management experience, while Lkhagvadorj misrepresented himself as a former Wall Street trader.
 Coinseed’s fraud totaled more than $1 million, according to James, who is also seeking restitution for investors.
 The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filed a related lawsuit against Coinseed and Davaasambuu over the tokens, which both regulators said were sold from December 2017 to May 2018.
  “I’m 100% sure that the suit is full of false accusations,” Davaasambuu said in an email, referring to James’ lawsuit. “It’s very strange that they’re blaming the very nature of how a startup works.”
  Both defendants have residences in Long Island City, New York, but Davaasambuu has expressed an intention to return to his native Mongolia, James said.
 “Unregulated and fraudulent virtual currency entities, no matter how big or small, will no longer be tolerated in New York,” James said in a statement.
  The attorney general said Coinseed had touted the CSD token as “a great opportunity for young people who want to make money in the crypto market,” and that the mobile app was “guaranteed” to help push its market value higher.
 “In truth, nearly three years later, the CSD token has not been listed anywhere,” her lawsuit said.
 James’ lawsuit in Manhattan Supreme Court alleged violations of the Martin Act, a New York state securities law. The SEC’s lawsuit is in Manhattan federal court.
title: 纽约州起诉关闭“欺诈性”硬币种子加密平台
title_en: New York Sues To Shut Down 'Fraudulent' Coinseed Crypto Platform

尽管人们越来越习惯于使用机器推荐的视频，但是许多用户仍然有主动搜索的冲动和需求。 Douyin对此有所了解，并于2018年中期合并了搜索功能。两年多后，该功能每月活跃用户达到5.5亿。斗隐的搜索功能仍有增长的空间，因为该应用程序于9月报告了6亿的每日用户，因此其每月用户群应高于此。
 年轻产品经理Kelly Zhang认为TikTok的中文版Douyin的崛起是本周首次在她的微博帐户上披露了Douyin的搜索用户数据。搜索是中国百度长期以来一直统治的领域。截至去年12月，百度的旗舰应用每月活跃用户为5.44亿，因此可以肯定地说，与百度相比，搜索豆音的人越来越多。
  张的讲话是在说明窦音在征服在线视频领域的雄心，并最终说明人们如何获得信息：“我以前曾说过：我希望窦隐能成为人类文明的视频百科全书。因此，视频搜索是这本书的索引，是寻找答案和收获新知识的门户。”
 她进一步补充说，随着视频应用继续加大对搜索功能的投资，斗音的搜索引擎正在招聘来年的研发，产品和运营角色（中国刚刚迎来农历）。
 数据分析公司吉光在去年12月的一份报告中说，短视频平台已经是中国用户在线搜索的第二大方法，仅次于百度等普通搜索引擎，并领先于社交网络和电子商务。百度搜索的命令越来越受到中国科技巨头建造的围墙花园的限制，这些花园花园互相封锁，阻止他们免费访问其站点和数据。现状会损害用户体验，但对于像Douyin和阿里巴巴的淘宝市场这样的应用程序中的垂直搜索引擎来说，则是一个好兆头，因此广告赞助收入也很高。
 ByteDance坚定不移地使用机器学习算法通过诸如Douyin，TikTok和新闻聚合器头条之类的服务推荐内容。该模型被证明是高效且有利可图的，促使其从百度到腾讯的前辈引入了类似的算法驱动的内容提要。 ByteDance进入搜索领域的历史悠久，是一个有趣而自然的步骤。该公司正在为其数字媒体帝国解决难题，为人们提供了另一种查找信息的选择。用户可以接收机器推荐，并根据需要订阅内容创建者。如果有一个很好的老方法，他们也可以输入搜索关键字。
note_en:
While people are increasingly used to being fed by machine-recommended videos, many users still have the urge and need for active searching. Douyin understood that and incorporated a search function back in mid-2018. More than two years later, the feature reached 550 million monthly active users. There’s still room for Douyin’s search feature to grow, as the app last reported  600 million  daily users in September, so its monthly user base should be above that.
 Kelly Zhang, the young product manager credited for the rise of Douyin, TikTok’s Chinese version,  disclosed Douyin’s search user figure for the first time this week on her microblogging account. Search is a territory that had long been dominated by Baidu in China. As of December, Baidu’s flagship app had 544 million monthly active users, so it’s safe to say as many people are searching on Douyin as on Baidu.
  Zhang’s remark is telling of Douyin’s ambition in conquering the online video sector, and eventually how people receive information: “I have said this before: I hope Douyin could become the video encyclopedia for human civilization. Video search is, therefore, the index of the book, the gateway to finding answers and reaping new knowledge.”
 She further added that Douyin’s search engine is hiring for research and development, product, and operational roles in the upcoming year (China has just observed the Lunar New Year) as the video app continues to ramp up investment in search capabilities.
 Short video platforms are already the second-most popular method for Chinese users to search online, trailing only after general search engines like Baidu and coming ahead of social networks and e-commerce, data analytics firm Jiguang  said in a report last December. Baidu’s command of search is increasingly limited by the walled gardens built up by Chinese tech titans who block one another from free access to its sites and data. The status quo harms user experience but bodes well for vertical search engines on apps like Douyin and Alibaba’s Taobao marketplace, and consequently revenues from ad sponsorships.
 ByteDance cut its teeth on using machine learning algorithms to recommend content through services like Douyin, TikTok and news aggregator Toutiao. The model proved highly efficient and lucrative, prompting its predecessors from Baidu to Tencent to introduce similarly algorithm-powered content feeds. ByteDance’s move into search, a realm with a longer history, is an intriguing yet natural step. The firm is just completing the puzzle for its digital media empire, giving people another option to find information. Users can receive machine recommendations and subscribe to content creators if they want. They can as well put in a search keyword if they have one in mind, the good old way.
title: TikTok的中国双胞胎Douyin拥有5.5亿搜索用户，取代了百度
title_en: TikTok’s China twin Douyin has 550 million search users, takes on Baidu

一夜之间，成千上万的人走上西班牙街头，以支持一个被囚的说唱歌手。
  帕勃罗·哈塞尔（Pablo Hasel）在为避免被捕而设在大学里之后，于周二被捕入狱。
 他因美化恐怖主义并在推特和歌曲歌词上诽谤王冠和国家机构而面临九个月的监禁。
   在哈塞尔开始服刑几个小时后，据报道在加泰罗尼亚地区的各个城市和东部城市瓦伦西亚都有示威活动。
   在巴塞罗那，大约有2,000人参加抗议活动并挥舞标语牌，上面写着＆＃34; Free Pablo＆＃34;。后来有报道称发生了一些暴力事件，警察迁入，示威者放下垃圾桶并扔了东西。
 最大的示威游行涉及赫罗纳市的5,000人，数百人聚集在莱里达（加泰罗尼亚的莱里达），说唱歌手于当天早些时候被捕。据加泰罗尼亚媒体报道，另有2,000人要求在其家乡Segrià释放Hasel。
   15名抗议者被捕，包括17名警察在内的33人受伤。巴塞罗那附近维克（Vic）的法院大楼遭到袭击，警察局遭到破坏。 
加泰罗尼亚警察部队成员莫索斯（Mossos）于周二早上在说唱歌手违反了上周五交出自己的截止日期后进入莱里达大学。
 他的支持者加入了大楼内的Hasel，在歌手被带走之前，向警察喷了灭火器，他们喊着“他们永远不会让我们沉默”。法西斯主义国家的死亡。
  包括电影导演佩德罗·阿尔莫多瓦（PedroAlmodóvar）和好莱坞巨星哈维尔·巴尔登（Javier Bardem）在内的200多位艺术家已经签署了反对哈塞尔（Hasel）入狱的请愿书，而国际特赦组织则将他的逮捕描述为西班牙言论自由的可怕消息。
  西班牙说唱歌手瓦尔托尼克（Valtònyc）在被裁定犯有类似罪行后于2018年逃往比利时，他告诉法新社，他感到羞耻＆＃34; &&&&&＃34;看到同事因艺术家的所作所为而受到这样的对待，这很惹恼。
 西班牙政府上周表示，计划减少对“表达罪行”的处罚。 -在涉及艺术或文化活动的情况下，包括颂扬恐怖主义，仇恨言论以及对王冠和宗教的侮辱。除了对君主制的袭击之外，帕勃罗·哈塞尔（Pablo Hasel）的推文和歌词还指控警察酷刑和杀害示威者和移民。
 帕勃罗·哈塞尔（Pablo Hasel）-本名帕勃罗·里瓦杜拉·杜罗（Pablo Rivadulla Duro）-在2014年的另一起案件中被判犯有美化恐怖主义罪，但他的刑期于2019年被暂停，条件是他三年之内不得再犯罪。
note_en:
Thousands of people have taken to the streets in Spain overnight in support of a jailed rapper.
  Pablo Hasel was taken to prison on Tuesday, after barricading himself in a university to avoid arrest.
 He faces nine months in prison for glorifying terrorism and slandering the crown and state institutions over tweets and song lyrics.
   Demonstrations were reported in cities across the Catalonia region and in the eastern city of Valencia on Tuesday night, hours after Hasel began serving his sentence.
   In Barcelona, around 2,000 people joined protests and waved placards reading "Free Pablo". Some violence was later reported on as police moved in and demonstrators set rubbish bins alight and threw objects.
 The biggest demonstration involved 5,000 people in the city of Girona while hundreds more gathered in Lérida (Lleida in Catalan), where the rapper was arrested earlier in the day. Another 2,000 people called for Hasel's release in his home town of Segrià, according to Catalan media.
   Fifteen protesters were arrested and 33 people were hurt including 17 police officers. The court building in Vic near Barcelona came under attack and the police station was damaged.
 Members of the Catalan police force, known as Mossos, entered the University of Lleida on Tuesday morning after the rapper defied a deadline to hand himself last Friday.
 His supporters, who had joined Hasel inside the building, sprayed fire extinguishers at police before the singer was led away, shouting "they will never silence us; death to the fascist state".
  More than 200 artists, including film director Pedro Almodóvar and Hollywood star Javier Bardem, have signed a petition against Hasel's jail term, while Amnesty International described his arrest as terrible news for freedom of expression in Spain.
  Fellow Spanish  rapper Valtònyc, who fled to Belgium in 2018 after being convicted of similar offences, told the AFP news agency he felt "shame" and "anger at seeing a colleague treated like this for doing what artists do, which is to provoke".
 The Spanish government said last week it planned to reduce the penalty for "crimes of expression" - including the glorification of terrorism, hate speech and insults to the crown and religion - in cases that involve artistic or cultural activities. Besides attacks on the monarchy, Pablo Hasel's tweets and lyrics accused police of torturing and killing demonstrators and migrants.
 Pablo Hasel - whose real name is Pablo Rivadulla Duro - was found guilty of glorifying terrorism in a separate case in 2014, but his prison sentence was suspended in 2019 on condition that he did not reoffend within three years.
title: 巴勃罗·哈塞尔（Pablo Hasel）抗议：成千上万要求说唱歌手在西班牙被释放
title_en: Pablo Hasel protests: Thousands demand rapper's release in Spain
